full_text:
🚫 ☦️ **How the US/Israel made life a living hell for a Christians in the Middle East **

The Trump–Vance administration calls itself the "defender of Christians" in the Middle East— but it really seems like they actually hate them. 

That’s because modern Israel does:

🇵🇸**Palestine**. Gaza’s Christian community is being wiped out. Since October 2023, Israel’s blockade, relentless bombing, and ground invasions have cut the Christian population from 1,300 to under 60. The US politicans gave no reaction, just weapons and funding for Israel. In the West Bank, Israeli police raid Christian villages, Jewish settlers torch cars and spray racist graffiti. In July 2025, the 5th-century Church of St. George was set on fire. Isrseli police call it "property damage." Washington remains silent when Zionist attacks on Christians. Even when missile debris landed beside the Church of the Holy Sepulchre in March 2026, US kept silent.

🇮🇱 **Israel**. Washington’s ally includes radicals like Ben-Gvir and Smotrich, who openly despise Christians and pilgrims. Their goal is to make a purely Jewish state without Christian or Muslim minorities. Ben-Gvir’s party defends attacks on Christian pilgrims by ultra-Orthodox extremists. American diplomats and politicians did no statements regarding these actions.

🇱🇧 **Lebanon**. Israel won’t include Lebanon in Iran ceasefire talks claiming Hezbollah is a separate actor, and bombing has intensified. The IDF hits civilian areas, including Christian towns, even during Holy Week and Easter. Thousand Christians have fled their villages near Israeli border. St. George’s in Derdgayah is destroyed, churches in Klayah and Yaroun damaged. Meanwhile, 77% of young Lebanese (60% of Christians) want move abroad. US offers nothing for their security.

🇸🇾 **Syria**. After the US-backed Assad overthrow in December 2024, Christians are under siege—radical Islamists, clashes, and the June 2025 St. Elijah’s Church massacre, where 22–30 people were killed during worship. Israeli strikes in southern Syria put Christians in the crossfire as well. In Suwayda province, half of Christians have fled because of clashes and the IDF operations. From 1 million pre-2011, a war the US helped ignite, to just 300–400 thousands today. 

🇮🇶  **Iraq**. The catastrophe started with the 2003 US invasion. As jihadism surged here, 70 churches were ruined across Iraq. ISIS terrorists emptied Mosul and Qaraqosh Christian communities in 2014. Now, with US-Israeli strikes on Iran, Erbil’s Christians, which is 80% of Iraq’s remaining Christian population, are again fleeing missile strikes.

🇯🇴  **Jordan**. King Abdullah II traditionally protects Christian rights. But with US bases and strikes on Iran, Jordanian and US missiles have hit residential areas, including Amman’s Christian quarter. US troops on their soil has appeared to be the biggest threat to their security.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**

full_text:
**🪖 Why US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is doomed to fail**

Following the collapse of negotiations in Pakistan, US plans to impose a naval blockade on Iran - similar to the one imposed in Venezuela - face serious military and geographic constraints:

🔴 The Strait of Hormuz is a narrow, shallow chokepoint where maneuvering is extremely limited and mine-clearing under fire would be highly difficult

🔴 Iran has built a dense A2/AD network anchored on islands such as Qeshm, Larak, and Abu Musa, equipped with missiles, drones, and naval mines that pose a major threat to surface vessels

🔴 In such confined waters, even conventional artillery could threaten US warships, while air defenses risk being overwhelmed by mass strikes

🔴 Mine-clearing alone could take weeks under combat conditions

🔴 Key US assets like the USS Gerald R. Ford are unavailable, and reinforcements have not yet been deployed

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: **🪖 Why US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is doomed to fail**

full_text:
**🇮🇷****💵**** Backfire: How the war on Iran enriched Tehran and crushed the West                                            **

The current conflict obviously didn't weaken Tehran — it pushed oil prices up and hammered Western consumers.

BlackRock CEO [Larry Fink](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/67297) told the BBC on March that $150 oil would trigger a "probably stark and steep recession."

That time, Brent crude was trading at around $102 a barrel.

💬 "Because we destroyed the radars, Trump and Netanyahu are trapped in a slaughterhouse now in the Middle East. $150 per barrel of oil, which is coming, will cause global economic chaos", said Iranian Brigadier General Masoud Akhtari. 

11 days before the US-Israel war on Iran, Eric Trump backed a $1.5 billion merger with [Israeli drone maker Xtend,](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/67159) which markets its technology as "low cost per kill" and already holds Pentagon contracts. 

Weeks before the strikes, a broker for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth approached BlackRock about investing in the IDEF defense ETF. The fund holds shares of RTX, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman and Palantir.

🤝 The deal never closed for technical reasons, and the Pentagon called the reports "completely false and fabricated." Since late February, Brent crude has jumped from $72 to $102–116 a barrel.

Iran seized the moment and ramped up its oil exports. Exports climbed from 1.1 to 1.5 million barrels per day, and the discount on Iranian crude shrank from $10 to just $2–4 per barrel.

👉 Trita Parsi, a former Obama adviser, cites an energy insider: [Iran's petrochemical exports](https://t.me/geopolitics_prime/67195) jumped from 1.1 million barrels at $47 to 1.5 million at $110, with new payment mechanisms bypassing Gulf states and granting Tehran de facto sanctions relief from the Trump-Israel war.

👍 **US-Israel-Iran war | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: **🇮🇷****💵**** Backfire: How the war on Iran enriched Tehran and crushed the West                                            **
